user_prompt: Seed: A young man gets a weekend job helping to clean out an old, abandoned house in his town. In a dusty cupboard, he finds an old video camera. The tape inside shows the exact room he is standing in fifty years ago, and the family is hiding a small metal box under the floorboards.

metadata.title: The Box Under the Floor
metadata.setting: A small, quiet town where a young man takes a weekend job cleaning out an old, abandoned house at the end of a narrow street.

characters.name: Marco
characters.description: A 19-year-old with dark hair and quick eyes who notices every small detail around him.
characters.flaw: Too curious — he always stops working to investigate things he finds interesting, which gets him into trouble.

characters.name: Mrs. Dale
characters.description: A woman in her sixties with grey hair and a tight, nervous smile who hired Marco to clean the house.
characters.flaw: Hides information instead of explaining it — she gives instructions but never gives reasons.

locations.name: The front hallway
locations.description: A dark, narrow entrance with peeling yellow paint on the walls and old newspapers on the floor.

locations.name: The back room
locations.description: A small, cold room at the back of the house with bare wooden floorboards, one dirty window, and a large cupboard against the far wall.

unresolved_threads: What is inside the metal box under the floorboards?
Is the box still under the floor after fifty years?
Why did Mrs. Dale tell Marco to stay in the kitchen and leave so quickly?
Who opened the front door at the end of the episode?

episode_plan.hook: Marco arrives at an old, abandoned house for his first day of work, but the woman who hired him leaves before he can ask a single question.
key_events: Mrs. Dale meets Marco at the front door, hands him a mop, and says 'Start in the kitchen — do not go to the back room'; she leaves before Marco can ask why, and the reader sees her hands are shaking as she locks her car.
Marco starts cleaning the kitchen, but he hears a soft knocking sound from the back room; he tells himself it is the wind, but his curiosity wins and he walks down the hall.
In the back room Marco finds a large, dusty cupboard and inside it an old video camera with a tape still inside; he spots an old television on the floor, connects the camera, and presses play.

The tape shows the same back room fifty years ago — a man and a woman hide a small metal box under the floorboards and then cover the boards again; Marco kneels down and touches the floor — but at that moment he hears the front door open.
episode_plan.ending_hook: Marco hides the camera behind his back and holds his breath — someone is standing in the hallway, but he cannot see who.

observations.narrative_quality: Strong. Therefore/but causality is clean throughout. Dramatic irony (Mrs. Dale's shaking hands) is well-crafted and does real work without requiring complex vocabulary.
observations.constraint_compliance: Good. Physical stakes, protagonist flaw drives plot, ending hook is tight.
observations.issues: Director placed 'video camera' and 'tape' in Marco's key_items before the episode begins — these are found during the episode, not before. State Manager should add them after the episode is written.
observations.vocabulary_targets: Appropriate for A2-mid. All six words arise naturally from plot events.
observations.overall: First Director run is production-quality. Prompt is working well — no iteration needed before moving to Writer prompt.
